artist.name: Rashmi
artist.tagline: A painter who paints from thought.
bio: Rashmi is a Bangalore-based painter with a background in fashion. She graduated from NIFT and spent a decade in the apparel industry before committing fully to paint. The eye she developed for colour, texture, and composition in fashion has carried into the work in a way that is immediately visible.

Her range is genuine. She moves across figurative and abstract with confidence, works across palette and scale, and can paint from a brief as easily as from a feeling. The art runs in the family — her father was a craftsman and painter who worked with his hands and never separated living from making.
She has shown work in two exhibitions in Allahabad, with near-complete sell-through at both. She takes commissions. She is based in Bangalore.

paintings.title: Fire
paintings.story: Sold at the Ladies Club Exhibition, Allahabad. Acquired by Hotel Yatrik.

paintings.title: Attraction
paintings.story: Sold at the Ladies Club Exhibition, Allahabad. Acquired by Dr. Vidula, one of the city's most respected gynaecologists.

paintings.title: Hope
paintings.story: April 2015 — the first painting. Deliberately not sold. A personal marker of the beginning of a journey.

paintings.title: Buddha on My Mind
paintings.story: Sold at Navya Exhibition, Allahabad, November 2015 — purchased while the stall was still being set up. Before the exhibition had even opened.

paintings.title: Space
paintings.story: Sold at Navya Exhibition, Allahabad, November 2015.

paintings.title: Cascading Hues
paintings.story: Sold at Navya Exhibition, Allahabad, November 2015.

paintings.title: The Quiet Window
paintings.story: A woman alone at a window — not waiting, not looking out at anything in particular. Just present. The light does what light does in the late afternoon. This is the piece that tends to stop people mid-conversation when they walk into a room where it is hanging.

paintings.title: Meditative I
paintings.story: Part of a two-piece meditative series. Both pieces sold through Facebook.

paintings.title: Meditative II
paintings.story: Part of a two-piece meditative series. Both pieces sold through Facebook.

paintings.title: Music Bliss I
paintings.story: Part of a two-piece series. Sold at the Ladies Club Exhibition, Allahabad. The set was so well received that a repeat order came in through Facebook shortly after.

paintings.title: Music Bliss II
paintings.story: Part of a two-piece series. Sold at the Ladies Club Exhibition, Allahabad. A repeat order of the full set followed through Facebook.

paintings.title: Motherhood
paintings.story: May 2015. Acquired by Mr. Atul Singh, retired Director of the Modi Group.

paintings.title: Jimmy Page
paintings.story: A commission from Sahil, a self-taught guitarist whose ideal is Jimmy Page, lead guitarist of Led Zeppelin. A portrait built from admiration.

paintings.title: Red City
paintings.story: A city on fire — or perhaps just alive. The skyline fractured into planes of red and amber, reflected in water that holds the whole thing upside down. Shown at exhibition in Allahabad. The title was written on the label before the paint was fully dry.

paintings.title: Pride
paintings.story: The peacock painted not as symbol but as pure visual event — that impossible blue, the eyes within eyes within eyes. A celebration of colour for its own sake. One of the more joyful pieces in the archive.

paintings.title: Wild
paintings.story: Three horses mid-motion against a deep red ground — angular, abstracted, full of contained energy. The gold and ochre against the crimson has the feeling of an old miniature painting pushed into something more urgent. Now in a private collection.

paintings.title: The Flute Player
paintings.story: Krishna rendered not as deity but as musician — the eyes closed, the body lost in the note being played. The blue skin, the yellow turban, the trishul mark on the forehead: the iconography is all there but worn lightly. What remains is the sound you almost hear.

paintings.title: Abhaya
paintings.story: The raised hand of blessing — the Abhaya mudra — rendered large enough to fill a room. Spirals mark the fingertips like energy pathways. The earth rests in the open palm. A piece about what it means to hold and to release simultaneously.

paintings.title: Behind the Mask
paintings.story: A figure in clown paint looks back over a shoulder wrapped in red — one eye visible, watchful, entirely human beneath the performance. There is nothing comic about this painting. It is about what we put on our faces so other people feel comfortable.

paintings.title: Ganesh — The Drummer
paintings.story: Part of a commissioned series of seven Ganesha interpretations. Each one was given a different aspect of the deity — this one got rhythm. The folk-art patterning on the dhoti, the cymbal mid-clash, the sheer joy of it. One of the most playful pieces in the series.

paintings.title: Ganesh — The Wanderer
paintings.story: From the same commissioned series of seven. This Ganesha walks and plays simultaneously — the flute to his lips, one foot lifted, the whole universe of figures and cattle woven into the fabric of his dhoti. Movement as devotion.

paintings.title: Abhaya — In Green
paintings.story: A second interpretation of the raised hand mudra — this time in deep forest green and yellow, more textured, more ancient-feeling. Where the first Abhaya holds warmth, this one holds something older. A Dharmachakra blooms in the open palm.
